title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Grey Hair
intro: Herbal teas for Grey hair are a natural remedy used to prevent, slow down, or even reverse the onset of premature greying of hair. These teas are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that promote hair growth, strengthen hair follicles, and reduce oxidative stress, which is a major contributor to grey hair. Some examples of herbal teas that can treat grey hair include Sage tea, which promotes hair growth and reduces stress; Rosemary tea, which improves circulation and stimulates hair follicles; Ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and promotes overall health; Turmeric tea, which contains curcumin, a powerful antioxidant that fights free radicals and promotes hair growth; Green tea, which is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties; and Black tea, which contains catechins that help reduce stress and promote hair growth. By incorporating these herbal teas into your diet, you can potentially slow down or reverse the onset of grey hair and promote a healthy, natural-looking mane.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, derived from the oat plant, have been traditionally used to address the issue of grey hair. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, help to nourish and rejuvenate the hair follicles, thus promoting healthier and more vibrant hair growth. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa, such as avenanthramides and avenacosides, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which include improving hair pigmentation and preventing premature greying. By using Avena sativa teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to stimulate hair growth, improve hair texture, and reduce the appearance of grey hair, ultimately promoting a more youthful and radiant appearance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Avena sativa in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea once or twice a day to promote healthy hair growth and reduce grey hair.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Die Pharmazie", Avena sativa teas for grey hair may be beneficial due to the presence of 7,8-dimethoxycoumarin (DMC), a natural coumarin molecule that has been shown to stimulate melanogenesis and activate melanin production in melanocytes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat grey hair, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin irritation or anaphylaxis in rare cases, as well as potential interactions with certain medications like blood thinners. To use Avena sativa teas safely, it's recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid taking it with other herbal preparations or medications that have a sedative effect to minimize the risk of drowsiness or other adverse effects.
preparation_usage: Start with 1 teaspoon of Avena sativa powder in 1 cup of warm water
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for 30 days
Gradually increase to 2 cups a day for the next 30 days
Maintain 2 cups of tea per day for optimal results
Continue for 3-6 months for noticeable reduction in grey hair

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat the grey hair ailment, also known as canities. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by protecting the hair follicles from oxidative stress and promoting the production of melanin. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum teas, including flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects, which help to treat this ailment by reducing the formation of free radicals and promoting the growth of new hair. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat this ailment include its potential to improve hair color, increase hair density, and promote overall hair health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. Use organic and pesticide-free plants.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the gathered flowers and leaves. Add them to a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers and leaves in the cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another cup. Discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a week to help promote hair growth and reduce grey hair. Store the tea in the fridge for up to 3 days.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to potentially treat grey hair, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with certain medications, particularly those for blood thinning and diabetes. Precautions to take when using Silybum marianum teas include avoiding excessive consumption, monitoring blood sugar levels, and being aware of potential interactions with other herbal remedies, as well as ensuring that the tea is prepared properly to avoid contamination.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea daily in the morning.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the tea to infuse.
Consume tea for 2-3 months to notice changes in grey hair.
Repeat tea consumption daily for 6 months for maximum effect.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat the grey hair ailment, known as canities, due to their potential to promote hair growth and reduce graying. The properties of this herbal preparation, such as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, help to treat this ailment by protecting the hair follicles from oxidative stress and promoting the growth of melanocytes, which are responsible for hair pigmentation. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, are responsible for their therapeutic effects, which may help to inhibit the activity of the enzyme responsible for hair graying. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat grey hair include promoting hair growth, reducing graying, and maintaining overall hair health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root (Curcuma longa) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the turmeric root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the turmeric root.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea once a day to help promote hair growth and reduce grey hair.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Die Pharmazie", Curcuma longa teas for grey hair may be beneficial due to the compound 7,8-dimethoxycoumarin present in Curcuma longa, which has been shown to increase melanin content and tyrosinase activity, potentially activating melanin production and preventing grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used as a potential treatment for grey hair, but consuming them may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Curcuma longa teas safely, it's essential to start with small doses and gradually increase the amount, as excessive consumption may lead to nausea and vomiting, and to avoid taking them for extended periods to minimize the risk of liver damage.
preparation_usage: Take 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder in a cup of warm water.
Mix well and drink the tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1 cup a day and increase to 2 cups as needed.
Continue using the tea for 3-6 months for noticeable results.
Combine with a healthy diet and regular exercise for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat the grey hair ailment, also known as canities or graying of hair, due to its potential to promote hair growth and improve scalp health. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are thought to help treat this ailment by improving blood circulation to the scalp, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting the conversion of melanin to eumelanin, the type of melanin responsible for hair color. These properties help to treat the grey hair ailment by promoting the growth of new hair and maintaining the health of existing hair follicles, thereby reducing the appearance of grey hair. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat the grey hair ailment include promoting hair growth, improving scalp health, and potentially reducing the risk of age-related hair loss.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat and add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to the tea infuser.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink immediately.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat the grey hair ailment, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Ginkgo biloba teas include being aware of potential interactions with blood thinners and other medications, and starting with low doses to monitor one's body's reaction.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea daily, morning or evening.
Take 1/2 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba extract powder daily.
Continue usage for 3-6 months for noticeable results.
Monitor your hair growth and adjust dosage as needed.
Consult a doctor before using Ginkgo biloba for grey hair.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, made from the fragrant leaves of the rosemary plant, are a popular herbal remedy for treating grey hair. This herbal preparation contains properties that help to stimulate hair growth and improve hair pigmentation, thus treating the grey hair ailment. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis, including carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and camphor, contribute to its ability to improve circulation and reduce oxidative stress in the scalp, promoting a healthy and natural hair color. The benefits of consuming Rosmarinus officinalis teas for treating grey hair include improved hair pigmentation, reduced graying, and a potential increase in the lifespan of hair follicles.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis (Rosemary) leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Rosemary leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat the process 2-3 times a week for desired results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can potentially cause gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, stomach upset, and diarrhea when consumed in large quantities to treat grey hair. To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, as well as to consume the tea in moderation and with adequate hydration, particularly for those with sensitive stomachs.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day.
Continue for 2-3 months for noticeable hair color improvement.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to address the issue of grey hair, with their properties believed to help stimulate hair growth and improve scalp health. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, including ginsenosides, panaxynol, and panaxydol, are thought to contribute to its potential hair-restoring effects by promoting blood flow to the scalp and reducing oxidative stress. The benefits of Panax ginseng teas in treating grey hair include improved hair density and reduced graying, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Additionally, Panax ginseng teas may also promote overall well-being and reduce the risk of age-related disorders, making them a potentially valuable addition to a grey hair treatment regimen.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the ginseng root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week to promote healthy hair growth and reduce grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to treat the grey hair ailment, but possible side effects include insomnia, headaches, and increased blood pressure due to its stimulating properties. To minimize the risk of side effects, it is recommended to consume Panax ginseng teas in moderation, ideally 2-3 cups per week, and to avoid using it with other stimulants, such as caffeine or energy drinks.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Panax ginseng tea daily for 2-3 weeks.
Take 200-300 mg of Panax ginseng extract daily in divided doses.
Gradually increase to 2 cups of tea or 400-600 mg of extract.
Continue using for 6-12 months to see noticeable results.
Monitor your progress and adjust dosage as needed with doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia auriculata teas have been traditionally used to treat grey hair, a common concern among individuals seeking to maintain a youthful appearance. The properties of Cassia auriculata teas that help to treat this ailment include its ability to promote melanin production and reduce stress, which are both contributing factors to premature greying. The bioactive constituents of Cassia auriculata teas, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, help to stimulate the activity of melanocytes, the cells responsible for producing melanin, thereby promoting hair pigmentation. Regular consumption of Cassia auriculata teas has been reported to help reduce grey hair, improve overall hair health, and promote a sense of well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for this common issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cassia auriculata flowers and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Boil the water and add the Cassia auriculata flowers. Let it boil for 5 minutes.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes. The mixture will start to darken.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Let the liquid cool down.
Once cooled, store the tea in an airtight container. To use, mix 1 teaspoon of the tea with 1 cup of water and drink twice a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can be used to treat grey hair, but they may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems in some individuals. When using Cassia auriculata teas, it is essential to take precautions to avoid overconsumption, as excessive use may lead to liver toxicity, and to choose a reputable source to ensure the quality and purity of the herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Take 1 teaspoon of Cassia auriculata powder in 1 cup of warm water.
Drink the mixture 2-3 times a day for 6-8 weeks.
Increase dosage to 2 teaspoons if grey hair is severe.
Use Cassia auriculata tea for 3-4 months for noticeable results.
Combine with a balanced diet and healthy lifestyle for optimal results.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been used to treat grey hair due to its potential to promote melanogenesis, the process of hair pigmentation. The properties of this herbal preparation, such as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, help to treat grey hair by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hair graying. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to stimulate the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color, thus helping to treat grey hair. The benefits of ginger tea in treating grey hair include its potential to promote healthy hair growth, reduce hair graying, and improve overall hair quality, making it a natural and promising remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of Zingiber officinale (ginger) root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Peel the ginger root and chop it into small pieces.
In a cup, add the chopped ginger pieces and pour the boiling water over them.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the ginger tea 2-3 times a week to help reduce grey hair appearance.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Die Pharmazie", Zingiber officinale teas for grey hair may be beneficial due to the presence of 7,8-dimethoxycoumarin (DMC), which has been shown to increase melanin content and tyrosinase activity in cells, thereby potentially activating melanin production and delaying the onset of grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a potential remedy for grey hair, but its effectiveness may vary from person to person, and some possible side effects include stomach upset, nausea, and increased heart rate due to the ginger's stimulant properties. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid consuming it before bedtime, and monitor your body's reaction to the herbal preparation, as excessive consumption can exacerbate existing conditions such as acid reflux or high blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder into a small bowl.
Mix 1 teaspoon of powder into 1 cup of warm water.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week for optimal results.
Use 1-2 cups of tea per week for 6-8 weeks for noticeable effects.
Combine Zingiber officinale tea with a balanced diet and healthy lifestyle.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been used to treat grey hair due to its potential to stimulate hair growth and improve scalp health. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of Melissa officinalis help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing stress and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, including rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid, have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-aging effects, which may contribute to its potential hair growth benefits. Regular consumption of Melissa officinalis teas may help to promote hair growth, reduce grey hair, and improve overall scalp health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Melissa officinalis leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for sweetness.
Drink the tea once a day, ideally before bedtime, for 2-3 months for potential benefits.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat grey hair, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small doses, avoiding it if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, and monitoring your body's response to the herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Use 1-2 teaspoons of Melissa officinalis tea powder per cup of hot water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally in the morning.
Continue drinking the tea for 6-8 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper nigrum teas, also known as black pepper tea, have been traditionally used to address the issue of grey hair due to its potential ability to promote melanin production and restore hair pigmentation. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including piperine, piperonal, and beta-carboline alkaloids, have been found to stimulate the production of melanin, leading to a reduction in grey hair. The antioxidants present in piper nigrum teas may also help to protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress, promoting healthy hair growth and preventing premature greying. Regular consumption of piper nigrum teas has been reported to promote natural hair pigmentation and potentially reduce grey hair, offering a natural and non-invasive solution to this common issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Piper nigrum leaves, 1 cup of water, and a tea strainer.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Add 1 tablespoon of Piper nigrum leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a strainer and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week for a month to see the results on grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper nigrum teas can be used to treat grey hair due to its potential to stimulate melanin production, but it may also cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. To minimize risks, it is recommended to use Piper nigrum teas in moderation and avoid consuming it if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergies, also be cautious when combining it with other medications or supplements.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Piper nigrum tea once daily for 6 months.
Use 1 teaspoon of Piper nigrum powder per cup of boiling water.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of Piper nigrum powder to your shampoo for 3 months.
Use Piper nigrum tea as a rinse for 5 minutes after shampooing twice weekly.
Mix 1/4 teaspoon of Piper nigrum oil with 1 cup of coconut oil for hair massage 2 times a week.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been used for centuries to help treat the grey hair ailment, also known as alopecia or hair loss. The properties of this herbal preparation include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which help to promote healthy hair growth and reduce the appearance of grey hair. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica teas, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, work together to stimulate hair growth, improve scalp health, and reduce stress, which are all contributing factors to the development of grey hair. The benefits of Urtica dioica teas in treating grey hair include promoting a natural and healthy hair growth cycle, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving overall scalp health, making it a popular natural remedy for those looking to reverse or slow down the onset of grey hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Urtica dioica leaves. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Use 1/4 cup of the Urtica dioica leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the Urtica dioica leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. Add 1 tablespoon of honey or lemon juice if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the Urtica dioica tea 2-3 times a week to help promote hair growth and reduce grey hair.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Die Pharmazie", Urtica dioica teas may be beneficial for grey hair due to its potential to stimulate melanin production and increase tyrosinase activity, as suggested by the study's findings on 7,8-dimethoxycoumarin, a compound also found in Urtica dioica.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat the grey hair ailment, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. To use Urtica dioica teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid taking it with other herbal preparations or medications, and be cautious when consuming it if you have a history of allergies or sensitive skin.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Urtica dioica tea 2-3 times a week.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
Continue for 6-12 months to see noticeable results.
Combine with a balanced diet and regular exercise.

preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas have been traditionally used to treat the grey hair ailment, and its properties such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities help to slow down the graying process. This herbal preparation is believed to help treat grey hair by stimulating the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color, and improving blood circulation to the scalp. The bioactive constituents of Centella asiatica teas, including asiatic acid, madecassic acid, and brahmoside, contribute to its therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Centella asiatica teas may help to promote healthy hair growth, reduce graying, and improve overall scalp health, making it a potential natural remedy for reversing grey hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Centella asiatica leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 2-3 minutes.
Add the Centella asiatica leaves to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week to help promote hair growth and reduce grey hair.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of ethnopharmacology, Centella asiatica teas for grey hair treatment are not mentioned, however Centella asiatica was the 6th most cited species in the study, with 36 mentions, used for heat-clearing and detoxification, and also to treat a large number of plant species were reported to treat various diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Centella asiatica teas can be used to treat grey hair due to its potential ability to stimulate hair growth, but possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Centella asiatica teas to treat grey hair, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it with other herbal preparations that may interact with its properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Withania somnifera teas have been traditionally used to treat the grey hair ailment, also known as premature graying or canities. The antistress and adaptogenic properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by reducing oxidative stress, promoting hair growth, and maintaining melanin production. The bioactive constituents of Withania somnifera teas, including withanolides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by protecting hair follicles from damage, promoting melanogenesis, and regulating hair growth. The benefits of Withania somnifera teas in treating grey hair include reduced stress levels, improved hair texture, and a more youthful appearance, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Withania somnifera root powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried Withania somnifera root powder to a cup and pour in the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired. You can also add other herbs like ginger or peppermint.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week for best results. Consult a doctor before using it as a treatment for grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Withania somnifera teas can cause mild side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for an extended period. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid consuming the tea close to bedtime to prevent disrupting sleep patterns.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been used to treat grey hair due to its ability to promote hair growth and reduce graying. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat the grey hair ailment by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme tyrosinase, which is responsible for converting melanin into its inactive form, leading to graying hair. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum perforatum, including hyperforin and hypericin, have been found to stimulate the hair follicles, increase melanin production, and promote hair growth, ultimately reducing the appearance of grey hair. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat grey hair include its natural and non-invasive approach, as well as its potential to promote overall hair health and reduce the risk of further graying.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers.
Use 1/2 cup of the dried flowers and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 2 cups of water in a kettle or pot.
Pour the boiling water over the dried flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, to help with grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and increased sensitivity to sunlight, in some individuals when used to treat grey hair. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses and monitor your body's response, avoid consuming Hypericum perforatum teas during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and discontinue use if you experience any adverse reactions, including headaches, dizziness, or stomach upset.

preparations.herb_desc: Bacopa monnieri teas have been traditionally used to treat the grey hair ailment, known as canities or graying of the hair, due to their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by promoting melanogenesis, the process of hair pigmentation, and reducing oxidative stress which contributes to hair graying. The bioactive constituents of Bacopa monnieri, including bacosides, brahmosides, and alkaloids, play a crucial role in treating grey hair by inhibiting the enzyme tyrosinase, which breaks down melanin, and increasing the production of melanin. The benefits of Bacopa monnieri teas in treating grey hair include reducing the rate of hair graying, promoting healthy hair growth, and improving overall hair quality, making it a potential natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Bacopa monnieri leaves.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot.
Add the dried Bacopa monnieri leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it warm or let it cool before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, Bacopa monnieri teas are not specifically mentioned for grey hair in the provided study, as it mainly discusses its potential in slowing down brain aging and enhancing memory.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Bacopa monnieri teas can help treat the grey hair ailment, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, bloating, and stomach discomfort, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes or itching. To use Bacopa monnieri teas safely, it's recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid taking it with other medications that slow down liver function, and be cautious of interactions with certain antidepressants and blood thinners.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the licorice root, have been used for centuries to address the issue of grey hair. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat the underlying causes of grey hair, such as oxidative stress and inflammation in the hair follicles. Bioactive constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contribute to their therapeutic effects, promoting hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hair. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been found to stimulate hair growth, improve hair texture, and reduce the visibility of grey hair, offering a natural and holistic solution to this common issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Use 1 tablespoon of the roots and chop them into small pieces.
Steep the chopped roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the roots. Let it cool down.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week to help promote hair growth and reduce grey hair.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Electronic physician, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for grey hair is not mentioned in the study, however, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as Licorice, was listed among the medicinal plants effective in the treatment of acute menopausal symptoms, but there is no mention of its use for grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to potentially treat grey hair due to its reported ability to stimulate melanin production, but it may cause side effects such as acne, dizziness, or irregular heartbeat due to its high glycyrrhizin content. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, including limiting the consumption to short periods to minimize potential interactions with medications and reducing the dosage to avoid excessive potassium loss.

preparations.herb_desc: Terminalia chebula teas, also known as Haritaki tea, have been traditionally used to treat the grey hair ailment due to its rejuvenating and restorative properties. The herbal preparation is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids that help to nourish and strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing greying. The antioxidants present in Terminalia chebula teas help to protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage, thereby promoting a healthy and youthful appearance. Regular consumption of Haritaki tea is believed to slow down the graying process and promote a natural, healthy hair color, making it a popular herbal remedy for addressing hair graying issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Terminalia chebula powder and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the Terminalia chebula powder to the cooled water and stir well.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid becomes dark brown.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, for desired results.
preparations.preparation_study: According to International journal of food sciences and nutrition, Terminalia chebula teas may be beneficial for grey hair as the fruits of T. chebula were found to be a rich source of gallic acid.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Terminalia chebula teas can be used to treat grey hair, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, or increased heart rate in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Terminalia chebula teas, such as starting with small doses, avoiding consumption during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and monitoring blood pressure and liver function regularly.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, derived from the peppermint plant, have been traditionally used to treat grey hair due to their stimulating and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by increasing blood circulation to the scalp, promoting hair growth, and reducing stress which is often linked to premature greying. The bioactive constituents of Mentha x piperita teas, including menthone, menthol, and limonene, play a crucial role in treating grey hair by inhibiting the production of melanin-inhibiting compounds and stimulating the melanocyte stem cells. The benefits of using Mentha x piperita teas for grey hair treatment include natural hair re-growth, improved scalp health, and a reduction in oxidative stress, making it a popular and effective herbal remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the peppermint leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the peppermint tea for taste.
Drink 1 cup of peppermint tea 2-3 times a week to promote healthy hair growth and reduce grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to potentially stimulate hair growth and reduce grey hair, but it may also cause gastrointestinal side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals due to its high menthol content. Precautions should be taken when using Mentha x piperita teas to treat grey hair, including avoiding excessive consumption, as it can lead to headaches, dizziness, and skin irritation in rare cases.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat grey hair, also known as canities or graying of hair. The herbal preparation's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and promote melanocyte stem cell proliferation, which in turn helps to treat grey hair by promoting hair pigmentation. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum zeylanicum, such as eugenol and cinnamaldehyde, have been found to stimulate the growth of melanocytes and increase melanin production, thereby contributing to the treatment of grey hair. Regular consumption of Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas has been found to promote hair pigmentation, reduce grey hair, and maintain healthy hair growth, making it a popular natural remedy for this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Take 1 tablespoon of Cinnamomum zeylanicum powder and add it to a tea infuser.
Steep the Cinnamomum zeylanicum powder in the cooled water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional).
Drink the tea once a day to help reduce grey hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas can be used to treat grey hair, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of low blood sugar levels due to the tea's potential to lower blood sugar. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, such as monitoring blood sugar levels, avoiding excessive consumption, and being cautious if you have a history of skin conditions or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat grey hair, also known as canities, due to their potential to stimulate melanogenesis and promote hair growth. The bioactive constituents present in Lavandula angustifolia, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, creating a favorable environment for hair pigmentation. The antioxidant properties of these compounds also help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, thereby promoting healthy hair growth and pigmentation. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas may lead to a reduction in grey hair, as well as other benefits such as improved skin health and reduced stress levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers into the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and discard the flowers.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a week to promote healthy hair growth.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat grey hair, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or over an extended period. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to consume Lavandula angustifolia teas in moderation, and to start with a small dose to gauge individual tolerance and sensitivity, especially if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking other medications.

intro_study: According to "American journal of clinical dermatology", teas for grey hair do not appear to be mentioned in this study as a treatment for grey hair, but it mentions that oral administration and topical application of antioxidant plant extracts (including green and black tea) can protect skin from UV-induced erythema, early aging, and irradiation-induced cancer, which might indirectly help with maintaining healthy hair.
